title: Labs: Detection Lab Writeup Template
content: Lab Context Platform: Example SOC lab Objective: Detect scripted credential dumping behavior Timebox: 90 minutes Approach Build timeline of suspicious process execution. Correlate command-line telemetry with parent-child process ancestry. Validate against known admin baselines to reduce false positives. Detection Output Document final output in three parts: Query or rule used Why it is resilient Where false positives may appear Reflection End each lab writeup with: One technique learned One detection improvement One follow-up test to run next time

title: Recent Attacks: New CVE Exploitation Triage Playbook
content: Why This Matters When exploitation details go public, the first 48 hours are critical for exposure mapping and compensating controls. Rapid Playbook Validate internet-facing exposure for the affected product. Identify vulnerable version ranges in asset inventory. Review logs for exploit chain indicators from disclosure date onward. Prioritize patching by business criticality and exploitability. Add temporary WAF or network controls where patching lags. Ransomware Lens Track if initial access brokers are discussing the CVE. Monitor lateral movement signs in the same asset groups. Escalate if backup management systems share exposure patterns. Deliverable Format For each CVE, publish: Exposure status Hunt query references Mitigation status Residual risk

title: APT Tracking: Campaign Delta Note (Q2 Snapshot)
content: Executive Summary This note captures shifts in initial access and C2 hosting behavior observed in a recurring intrusion cluster. Observed Changes Initial access shifted from macro-heavy lures to archive-plus-script delivery. C2 moved to shorter-lived VPS hosts with domain rotation every few days. Post-exploitation favored native admin tools over dropping many binaries. ATT&CK Highlights T1566 Phishing T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter T1071 Application Layer Protocol Analyst Notes The key tracking metric is not volume of IOCs but stability of operator behavior over time. Keep one-page delta summaries for every campaign checkpoint.

title: Infra Hunting: Fast-Flux Cluster Triage Workflow
content: TL;DR This workflow starts from one suspicious domain and expands quickly using passive DNS, TLS overlap, and hosting artifacts to identify related infrastructure. Starting Artifact Seed domain: update-security-check[.]com Trigger: unusual DNS churn and short TTL values Pivot Strategy Collect passive DNS history for the last 30 days. Group by recurring IPs and ASN. Pivot into certificates reused across adjacent domains. Score candidates by overlap count and recent activity. What Worked Shared certificate Subject Alternative Name overlap was the strongest signal. WHOIS fields were low value due to privacy masking. Detection Opportunity Alert on domains with: TTL under 300 seconds over repeated windows More than 5 unique A records in 24 hours Certificate overlap with known suspicious cluster
